calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-04-2024 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Revoke Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Reid Harada appeared by zoom for Jennifer Greenhalgh.
Jelena Kelleher appeared by zoom for and with Kate Thornton (executor) by zoom.

The court noted that there is a question about whether this is a will or not, and whether an evidentiary hearing is going to be needed, and stated that it thinks this is a legal question. Mr. Harada stated that he agrees that this is a legal question. Mr. Harada and Mrs. Kelleher indicated that they are ready to submit on the matter. The Petition to Revoke Probate of Will is under submission. The court will prepare a form of order to reflect its ruling.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Revoke Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Jun-03-2024 Continued To Jul-29-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jelena Kelleher appeared by zoom for and with Kate Thornton (personal representative) by zoom.
Reid Harada appeared by zoom for Jennifer Greenhalgh (petitioner).

Mr. Harada stated that he is okay with allowing any filings and objections. Discussion was held with regard to whether there was clear and convincing evidence that the testator intended his letter to be his will, the process the court took when reviewing Mrs. Thornton's petition, the contradictory sentence in the document, whether or not an evidentiary hearing will be requested, the need to allow time for discovery, etc. The Petition to Revoke Probate of Will is continued to July 29, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow the parties time to file additional briefing. A memorandum of points and authorities in support of the petition is to be filed on or before June 20, 2024. An opposition memo is to be filed on or before July 15, 2024. Any reply is to be filed on or before July 22, 2024.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-04-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Jelena Kelleher, Esq., for and with Kate Thornton, Petitioner.

Upon query by the court, counsel confirms efforts to locate heirs at law. The court confirms that it is satisfied with counsel's report and confirms that there are no appearances to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Oct-29-2019 is admitted to probate. Kate Thornton is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Frank Mahoney III is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Jan-27-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Pliego/Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Continued To Dec-04-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jelena Kelleher appeared by zoom for and with Kate Thornton by zoom.

The court stated that the concern is with whether the heirs at law are being properly handled and noted that they reduced the pool of people who should be noticed down to 3 from 28. Mrs. Kelleher stated that the heir search company is very reluctant to give them information regarding who they might find, but they still need addresses, that the latest proposal from the heir search company is $7,000.00 to produce an affidavit of their efforts/what they have done so far (10 weeks minimum), and that the house is worth about 1.5 million and needs quite a bit of work. Mrs. Thornton stated that the electrical and the plumbing are in need of re-doing. Mrs. Kelleher stated that even if they find someone, they would not be eligible to serve, asked that Mrs. Thornton be appointed, and raised the issue of the court finding that the letter is a will, and that Mrs. Thornton is the only heir. Mrs. Kelleher also raised the issue of doing publication in Rotterdam. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to December 4, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Bastain. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
